title: VIJAY THALAPATHY WON, BUT CAN'T BECOME CM?
description:
Thalapathy Vijay’s TVK has emerged as the single-largest party in the Tamil Nadu Assembly Election, but Vijay is still unable to become Chief Minister. TVK won 108 seats, while the majority mark in Tamil Nadu is 118 seats. Even after Congress support, Vijay still needs more MLAs to prove majority. This has created a hung assembly situation, where smaller parties like VCK, Left parties and IUML can become kingmakers. Now the big question is: will Governor Rajendra Arlekar give Vijay the first chance to form the government, or will DMK and AIADMK try to build an alternative government?

Watch this full video to understand the Tamil Nadu political drama, TVK government formation crisis, Vijay CM issue, hung assembly meaning, majority mark, Governor’s role, and what can happen next in Tamil Nadu politics. Recent reports also highlight that TVK is short of the 118-seat majority mark and the Governor has asked for proof of support.
